nodes.name: Astronomy
nodes.description: Solar system, stars, galaxies, and the cosmos

config.prompt: Name the 8 planets of the solar system in order from the Sun

items.explanation:
Closest planet to the Sun; orbital period: 88 Earth days.
Smallest planet; no moons; surface swings from 430°C (day) to −180°C (night) — no atmosphere to retain heat.
Named for the Roman messenger god.
Explored by Mariner 10 (1974–75) and MESSENGER (2011–2015).

Hottest planet at ~465°C — hotter than Mercury despite being farther from the Sun.
Dense CO2 atmosphere creates a runaway greenhouse effect; atmospheric pressure 92× Earth's.
Rotates retrograde (backward); a day on Venus is longer than its year.
Brightest natural object in the sky after the Moon.

Third planet; only known planet with life.
One natural satellite: the Moon.
Surface ~71% water; atmosphere ~78% nitrogen, ~21% oxygen.
Orbital period: 365.25 days (hence leap years every 4 years).

The Red Planet — colored by iron oxide (rust) on its surface.
Two small moons: Phobos and Deimos.
Has Olympus Mons, the largest volcano in the solar system (~22 km high, 3× the height of Everest).
Currently explored by NASA's Perseverance rover (landed February 2021).

Largest planet — more than twice the mass of all other planets combined.
97 confirmed moons (as of 2025), including the four Galilean moons: Io, Europa, Ganymede, Callisto.
The Great Red Spot is a storm larger than Earth, raging for centuries.
A gas giant; no solid surface.

Famous for its spectacular ring system of ice and rock particles.
274 confirmed moons (as of 2025), the most of any planet; Titan has a thick nitrogen atmosphere.
Least dense planet — less dense than water; would float if placed in a large enough ocean.
The rings are mostly less than 1 km thick but extend up to 282,000 km from the planet.

An ice giant with a faint ring system.
Rotates on its side — axial tilt of ~98°, so it effectively rolls around the Sun.
27 known moons, all named after characters from Shakespeare and Alexander Pope.
The coldest planetary atmosphere in the solar system (−224°C recorded).

Farthest planet from the Sun; orbital period: 165 Earth years.
16 known moons; Triton is the largest and orbits backward — likely a captured Kuiper Belt object.
Predicted mathematically before it was observed (1846), based on perturbations in Uranus's orbit.
Has the strongest sustained winds in the solar system — up to 2,100 km/h.
